circulatory.a: Thoracic cavity, mediastinum, slightly left of midline
circulatory.g: 4-chambered pump — 2 atria (receiving) + 2 ventricles (pumping), divided by interventricular septum
circulatory.x: Heart has 4 chambers: RA, RV, LA, LV. Left ventricle wall is thickest — pumps blood to entire body. Pulmonary artery carries deoxygenated blood — most tested NEET trap.

circulatory.a: Upper left abdomen, behind stomach, below diaphragm
circulatory.g: Largest lymphoid organ — red pulp filters blood, white pulp is immune tissue
circulatory.x: Spleen destroys old RBCs via Kupffer-like macrophages — called the graveyard of RBCs. Also stores platelets. Ruptured spleen = medical emergency. Classic NEET MCQ.

nervous.a: Cranial cavity, protected by skull and meninges
nervous.g: 3 regions — cerebrum (largest, 85%), cerebellum (balance), brainstem (vital functions); ~86 billion neurons
nervous.x: Forebrain: cerebrum, thalamus, hypothalamus. Midbrain: visual/auditory reflexes. Hindbrain: cerebellum, pons, medulla. Hypothalamus regulates body temperature — classic NEET MCQ.

nervous.a: Vertebral canal, from medulla oblongata to L1/L2 vertebra
nervous.g: H-shaped grey matter (cell bodies) surrounded by white matter (myelinated fibres) — 31 pairs of spinal nerves
nervous.x: Grey matter = H-shaped in cross section, contains cell bodies. White matter = myelinated axons conducting impulses. Reflex arc bypasses brain — receptor → spinal cord → effector. NEET favourite.

respiratory.h: Gas exchange — O₂ in, CO₂ out
respiratory.a: Thoracic cavity, flanking the heart on both sides
respiratory.g: Right lung has 3 lobes, left lung has 2 lobes (cardiac notch). ~300 million alveoli — total surface area ~70 m²
respiratory.x: Right lung = 3 lobes (superior, middle, inferior). Left lung = 2 lobes (cardiac notch accommodates heart). Alveoli = functional units of gas exchange. Breathing triggered by rising CO₂ via chemoreceptors in medulla oblongata.

digestive.g: Largest gland — 4 lobes (right, left, caudate, quadrate); dual blood supply: portal vein + hepatic artery
digestive.x: Liver is the largest GLAND (skin = largest organ). Functions: bile production, detoxification, glycogen storage, urea synthesis. Kupffer cells destroy old RBCs — classic NEET MCQ.

digestive.a: Retroperitoneal, behind stomach, between duodenum and spleen
digestive.g: Mixed gland — exocrine (acinar cells → digestive enzymes) + endocrine (Islets of Langerhans → insulin/glucagon)
digestive.x: Islets of Langerhans: α cells → glucagon (raises blood sugar), β cells → insulin (lowers blood sugar). Destruction of β cells → Type 1 diabetes. Most tested NEET endocrine MCQ.

digestive.g: 5 parts: caecum (+ appendix), ascending colon, transverse colon, descending colon, sigmoid colon → rectum → anus
digestive.x: Large intestine absorbs water and electrolytes. No digestive enzymes secreted. Appendix = vestigial lymphoid organ attached to caecum. Gut bacteria synthesise Vitamin K and B12 here.

digestive.h: Primary site of digestion and absorption
digestive.a: Central abdominal cavity, surrounded by large intestine
digestive.g: 3 parts: duodenum (25 cm) → jejunum (2.5 m) → ileum (3.5 m); villi + microvilli increase surface area ~600×
digestive.x: Small intestine = main site of digestion AND absorption. Villi + microvilli (brush border) give surface area of ~200 m². Duodenum receives bile (liver) + pancreatic juice. Ileum absorbs Vitamin B12 + bile salts. NEET high yield.

excretory.a: Retroperitoneal, posterior abdominal wall, T12–L3 level
excretory.g: Each kidney contains ~1 million nephrons — functional unit; outer cortex + inner medulla + renal pelvis structure
excretory.x: Nephron: Bowman's capsule → PCT → Loop of Henle → DCT → Collecting duct. ADH acts on collecting duct — water reabsorption. Aldosterone acts on DCT — Na⁺ reabsorption. Most tested NEET excretion concept.

excretory.a: Retroperitoneal, slightly lower than left kidney (displaced by liver)
excretory.g: Right kidney sits slightly lower than left due to liver. Same nephron structure — ~1 million nephrons per kidney
excretory.x: Right kidney is lower than left kidney — liver pushes it down. Both kidneys together filter ~180L of blood per day producing ~1.5L urine. Renal threshold for glucose = 180 mg/dL.

excretory.g: Smooth muscle wall (detrusor muscle) — expands to store urine; trigone region at base has 3 openings (2 ureters + urethra)
excretory.x: Detrusor muscle contracts during micturition (urination). Internal urethral sphincter = involuntary (smooth muscle). External urethral sphincter = voluntary (skeletal muscle). Micturition reflex centre = sacral spinal cord (S2–S4).
excretory.note: GLB may need redownload — 194KB is unusually small
